# Threat Model

Generated by `/gaia-threat-model` for the AcmeAPI service.

sidecar_decision: security-sidecar/threat-model-decisions.md

## Assets

| Asset | Component | Sensitivity |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| User credentials | auth-service | critical |
| PII records | user-service | high |

## STRIDE Analysis

### Component: auth-service

- Spoofing — Attacker forges a bearer token to impersonate a user.
- Tampering — JWT payload is modified in transit to escalate privileges.
- Information Disclosure — Credentials leak in error logs.
- Denial of Service — Brute force on login endpoint saturates the service.
- Elevation of Privilege — Regular user gains admin role via a bug in claims.

### Component: user-service

- Spoofing — API accepts unsigned requests from an internal caller.
- Tampering — PII record modification bypasses audit trail.
- Repudiation — Deletion of account is not logged.
- Information Disclosure — Broken access control returns other users' PII.
- Denial of Service — Unbounded query on the users endpoint.
- Elevation of Privilege — Horizontal privilege escalation via IDOR.

## DREAD Scoring

| Threat | D | R | E | A | D | Risk |
|--------|---|---|---|---|---|------|
| Forged bearer token | 8 | 7 | 6 | 9 | 5 | High |
| JWT tampering | 9 | 6 | 5 | 9 | 4 | High |
| Credential leak via logs | 8 | 8 | 9 | 9 | 7 | Critical |

## Mitigations

| Threat | Mitigation |
|--------|------------|
| Forged bearer token | Enforce asymmetric JWT signatures with a rotating key set. |
| JWT tampering | Validate signature server-side on every request. |
| Credential leak via logs | Redact sensitive fields via a structured logger allowlist. |

## Security Requirements

- SR-1 — Bearer tokens must be signed with RS256.
  - AC: The gateway rejects tokens with `alg: none`.
- SR-2 — Sensitive fields must be redacted from application logs.
  - AC: Log output contains no email or API-key value when replayed.
